repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
TODO:

 - [x] implement proper window option. for demo testing use `nvim__win_set_bar(win, true, "statusline style string")`
 - [x] work correctly in a float
 - [x] support mouse click like tabline (and/or just general callback)
 - [ ] investigate compat with vim8 winbar (perhaps can be pollyfilled by some lua code)

comment_regular_data.body:
Hello,
Could you show what it looks like or the kind of things it'll allow to do?

@bew I tried this pr, it's cool. can be used to show the document symbol line like vscode :)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47070852/155141151-91e5c102-f871-4b73-82a1-48ce43001643.png)

so is it a statusline at the top basically?
also
how can I test out prs?

Currently, it seems to be a statusline under the tabline. 
Check out pr and build from source @max397574

> Check out pr and build from source @max397574

I've never done that before
is there like a guide for it somewhere
or which commands do I need to run?

can I just use this?
```
sudo apt-get install ninja-build
make distclean
make  # Nvim build system uses ninja automatically, if available.
```
You can use GitHub CLI and `gh pr checkout`, then build.
does this enable both global stautsbar and window bar?
No. Global statusbar is enabled by `set laststatus=3`.

> No. Global statusbar is enabled by `set laststatus=3`.

Yes, I know, maybe my questio was not clear. Will be possible to have a global status bar and, as provided by this PR, an individual bar for each window after this PR is merged?
Support for 'winbar' option was completed by @famiu and merged as #18562 . Support for clickable areas and vim9bar is still planned, this is open as a tracking issue in the meanwhile.

@xiyaowong cuz `winbar` won't update automatically with the cursor move, thus I think it is not a good place to show the document symbols and any other contents that need to update when cursor moves.  

Hello @bfredl , do you think if `winbar` is a good place for such contents? Why not make it behave like `statusline`, i.e., update automatically when the cursor moves? Thank you very much.
@rockyzhang24 That's definitiely a bug. winbar can show the same contents as statusline, and need to be redrawn by the same external events.

@bfredl Thank you. I tried to use a counter to see whether winbar will update when I move the cursor

```
let g:cnt = 0
function AddOne()
  let g:cnt = g:cnt + 1
  echom g:cnt
endfunction

set winbar=%{AddOne()}
```

The counter won't update with the cursor movement. Next, I tested it for statusline and the counter updated as expected. 

Should I open a new issue for this bug? Thanks. 

---

Update:
Hi @bfredl , I already opened a new issue regarding the winbar update (https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/18645). Thank you.

> TODO:
> 
>     * [x]  implement proper window option. for demo testing use `nvim__win_set_bar(win, true, "statusline style string")`
> 
>     * [x]  work correctly in a float
> 
>     * [ ]  support mouse click like tabline (and/or just general callback)
> 
>     * [ ]  investigate compat with vim8 winbar (perhaps can be pollyfilled by some lua code)

@bfredl I think we can check the 3rd item too now that #18650 is merged

> Support for 'winbar' option was completed by @famiu and merged as #18562 .

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/17336#issuecomment-1129876133

> Support for 'winbar' option was completed by @famiu and merged as #18562 . Support for clickable areas and vim9bar is still planned, this is open as a tracking issue in the meanwhile.

We have winbar now with clickable areas now, do we still need to keep this PR around?

title: winbar
